description: Systematically run through all E2E test flows from E2E_TEST_FLOWS.md using the chrome-devtools MCP browser tools. Each story covers a section of E2E flows — navigate the app at localhost:5173, execute each test step, verify expected behavior via snapshots/screenshots, and fix any issues found. Write results to ralph/activity.md.

userStories.title: E2E: Authentication flows (Section 1)
userStories.description: As a QA tester, I want to verify all authentication flows so that login, logout, redirects, and error states work correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: 1.1 Login: Navigate to /dashboard, verify redirect to login, enter dev@example.com / test123, click Sign In, verify redirected to /dashboard with user avatar visible
1.2 Login Failure: Enter wrong password, verify toast error 'Sign in failed' appears, user stays on login page
1.3 Already Authenticated Redirect: While logged in, navigate to /, verify auto-redirect to /dashboard
1.6 Sign Out: Click user menu, click Sign Out, verify redirected to login page
Log all pass/fail results with screenshots to ralph/activity.md
If any test fails, diagnose the root cause and fix the code, then re-verify
userStories.notes: Use chrome-devtools MCP tools: navigate_page, take_snapshot, fill, click, wait_for, take_screenshot. Credentials: dev@example.com / test123. Base URL: http://localhost:5173. Skip tests 1.4 (session expiry - requires waiting/manual cookie clearing) and 1.5 (access denied - requires a second non-allowed user). Take a screenshot after each verification for the activity log. Always start by navigating to the login page fresh. After login, use take_snapshot to find elements by uid.

userStories.title: E2E: Landing page & 404 (Section 2)
userStories.description: As a QA tester, I want to verify the landing page content and 404 page so that unauthenticated users see the correct experience.
acceptanceCriteria: 2.1 Landing Page: Sign out first, navigate to /, verify hero section with title, wheel visualization, 'How it Works' section, sign-in button visible
2.2 404 Page: Navigate to /nonexistent, verify 404 / Not Found page displayed
Log all pass/fail results with screenshots to ralph/activity.md
If any test fails, diagnose the root cause and fix the code, then re-verify
userStories.notes: Must be logged out first to see landing page (logged-in users redirect to /dashboard). Use take_snapshot to verify page content. Sign out by navigating to the app, finding the user menu, clicking Sign Out. If already logged out, landing page loads directly.

userStories.title: E2E: Dashboard & Navigation (Section 3)
userStories.description: As a QA tester, I want to verify dashboard navigation, tabs, header stats, and deep links so that the core navigation works correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: 3.1 Header Stats: Log in, verify header shows 'N Club Events' and 'N Club Recipes' badges
3.2 Tab Navigation: Click Home, Events, Recipes, Meals tabs, verify URL updates to /dashboard, /dashboard/events, /dashboard/recipes, /dashboard/meals respectively
3.4 Deep Links: Navigate directly to /dashboard/events, /dashboard/recipes, /dashboard/meals, verify correct tab is active each time
3.6 Home Tab - Admin View: Verify Home tab shows Ingredient Wheel and/or Ingredient Bank sections
Log all pass/fail results with screenshots to ralph/activity.md
If any test fails, diagnose the root cause and fix the code, then re-verify
userStories.notes: Login first with dev@example.com / test123. Use take_snapshot to verify tab states and content. The dev user is an admin, so 3.6 applies. Skip 3.3/3.5 (mobile viewport) for now — those are covered in US-015 responsive design. Skip 3.7 (non-admin view) as we only have one test user.

userStories.title: E2E: Ingredients & Wheel (Section 4)
userStories.description: As a QA tester, I want to verify ingredient bank management and wheel spinning so that the core event-creation flow works.
acceptanceCriteria: 4.1 Add Ingredient: Navigate to Home tab, find ingredient bank, enter a test ingredient name, click Add, verify it appears in the bank list
4.3 Duplicate Prevention: Try adding the same ingredient again, verify validation prevents duplicate
4.4 Remove Ingredient: Click remove on the test ingredient, verify it's removed from the bank and count decrements
Verify the ingredient count displays correctly (e.g., 'N/10')
Log all pass/fail results with screenshots to ralph/activity.md
If any test fails, diagnose the root cause and fix the code, then re-verify
userStories.notes: Must be logged in as admin (dev@example.com). Navigate to /dashboard (Home tab). Use take_snapshot to find the ingredient input field, add button, and bank list. Use a unique test ingredient name like 'E2E-Test-Ingredient-[timestamp]' to avoid conflicts. Skip 4.2 (autocomplete requires an out-of-bank ingredient), 4.5/4.6/4.7 (bank full and wheel spin are destructive — they create events and modify bank state heavily). Clean up by removing the test ingredient after verification.

userStories.title: E2E: Events List & Event Detail header (Sections 14 + 6.1)
userStories.description: As a QA tester, I want to verify the Events list tab and event detail page navigation/header so that event browsing works correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: 14.1 Upcoming Events: Navigate to /dashboard/events, verify scheduled events are listed with ingredient name, date, time, recipe count
14.2 Past/Completed Events: Verify completed events listed below upcoming events
14.3 Event Card Navigation: Click on an event card, verify navigates to /events/{id}
6.1 Event Detail Header: On event detail page, verify back button exists, ingredient name as heading, event date/time/recipe count shown
Log all pass/fail results with screenshots to ralph/activity.md
If any test fails, diagnose the root cause and fix the code, then re-verify
userStories.notes: Login first. Navigate to /dashboard/events. Use take_snapshot to find event cards. Click the first event card to navigate to its detail page. If no events exist, note this in the activity log as 'SKIP — no events in test data'. The event detail page URL pattern is /events/{uuid}.

userStories.title: E2E: Event Detail - Recipes Tab (Section 6.2, 6.7, 6.8, 6.10)
userStories.description: As a QA tester, I want to verify the event detail recipes tab, recipe cards, adding/editing recipes, and external links.
acceptanceCriteria: 6.2 Recipes Tab: On an event detail page, verify recipe count heading, recipe cards show name/author/rating/notes
6.7 Add Recipe Manually: Click 'Add Recipe', enter a test recipe name (no URL), verify recipe created and appears in list
6.8 Edit Recipe: Click edit on the test recipe, change the name, save, verify updated
6.10 External Link: If a recipe has a URL, verify external link icon visible in header row with aria-label 'Open recipe URL'
Clean up: Delete the test recipe after verification
Log all pass/fail results with screenshots to ralph/activity.md
If any test fails, diagnose the root cause and fix the code, then re-verify
userStories.notes: Navigate to an event detail page (from US-005 or directly to a known event URL). Use take_snapshot to find the 'Add Recipe' button. For 6.7, just create a manual recipe (no URL) to keep it simple. For editing, click the pencil icon on the card. After tests, delete the test recipe to clean up. If no events exist, note 'SKIP' in activity log.

userStories.title: E2E: Event Detail - Delete Recipe & Grocery Tab (Sections 6.9, 6.11, 6.13)
userStories.description: As a QA tester, I want to verify recipe deletion with confirmation and the grocery tab layout including pantry filtering.
acceptanceCriteria: 6.9 Delete Recipe: First add a test recipe, then click delete, verify confirmation dialog appears, confirm deletion, verify recipe removed from list
6.11 Grocery Tab: Click 'Grocery' tab, verify combined list visible with items grouped by category
6.13 Pantry Filtering: On Grocery tab, verify pantry items excluded, verify info banner about excluded items
Log all pass/fail results with screenshots to ralph/activity.md
If any test fails, diagnose the root cause and fix the code, then re-verify
userStories.notes: Navigate to an event detail page. For 6.9, create a temporary recipe first (manual, no URL), then delete it. For grocery tab, if no recipes have parsed ingredients, the grocery tab may be empty — note this as expected behavior. Take snapshots of the grocery tab to verify category grouping.

userStories.title: E2E: Event Detail - Pantry Tab & Export (Sections 6.14, 6.15)
userStories.description: As a QA tester, I want to verify the pantry tab CRUD and grocery export functionality on the event detail page.
acceptanceCriteria: 6.15 Pantry Tab: Click 'Pantry' tab, verify pantry items displayed, add a test item, verify toast 'Added [item] to pantry', remove the test item via trash icon, verify confirmation dialog, confirm removal
6.14 Grocery Export: Click 'Grocery' tab, find export menu, verify 'Copy to Clipboard' and 'Download CSV' options exist
Log all pass/fail results with screenshots to ralph/activity.md
If any test fails, diagnose the root cause and fix the code, then re-verify
userStories.notes: Navigate to an event detail page. For pantry tab, use a unique item name like 'e2e-test-pantry-item' to avoid conflicts. After adding, remove it to clean up. For export, just verify the buttons exist (don't need to actually download — hard to verify file downloads via browser tools).

userStories.title: E2E: Recipe Hub - Club & Personal tabs (Section 7.1-7.4, 7.6-7.8, 7.12)
userStories.description: As a QA tester, I want to verify the Recipe Hub tabs, search, filter, sort, and empty states.
acceptanceCriteria: 7.1 Club Recipes: Navigate to /dashboard/recipes, verify 'Club' tab shows recipes with name, creator, ingredient tag, rating, notes count
7.2 Personal Recipes: Click 'My Recipes' tab, verify personal recipes shown with 'Personal' badge, edit/delete icons visible
7.3 Search by Name: Type a recipe name in search bar, verify results filtered
7.6 Filter by Ingredient: On Club tab, click ingredient filter dropdown, select an ingredient, verify filtered results
7.7 Sort Options: Test sort dropdown — Alphabetical, Highest Rated, Newest First
Log all pass/fail results with screenshots to ralph/activity.md
If any test fails, diagnose the root cause and fix the code, then re-verify
userStories.notes: Navigate to /dashboard/recipes. Use take_snapshot to find tab buttons, search input, filter dropdown, sort dropdown. For search, type the first few characters of a recipe name visible in the snapshot. For filter/sort, click the dropdowns and select options. If no club recipes or personal recipes exist, note in activity log.

userStories.title: E2E: Recipe Hub - Edit/Delete recipes (Section 7.8-7.11)
userStories.description: As a QA tester, I want to verify adding, editing, and deleting recipes from the Recipe Hub.
acceptanceCriteria: 7.8 Add Personal Recipe: On My Recipes tab, click 'Add Recipe', enter name, verify recipe appears in list
7.9 Edit Recipe: Click edit (pencil) on the new recipe, change the name, save, verify updated
7.10 Delete Recipe: Click delete (trash) on the test recipe, verify confirmation dialog, confirm, verify recipe removed
7.11 Edit Ingredients button: Verify Edit Ingredients (ListChecks) icon visible on recipes you created
Log all pass/fail results with screenshots to ralph/activity.md
If any test fails, diagnose the root cause and fix the code, then re-verify
userStories.notes: Navigate to /dashboard/recipes and click 'My Recipes' tab. Create a test recipe with a unique name like 'E2E Test Recipe [timestamp]'. After editing and verifying, delete it to clean up. For 7.11, just verify the ListChecks icon button exists on a recipe card you created (don't need to open the dialog — that's tested elsewhere).

userStories.title: E2E: Recipe Notes CRUD (Section 8)
userStories.description: As a QA tester, I want to verify adding, viewing, editing, and deleting recipe notes on the event detail page.
acceptanceCriteria: 8.1 Add Notes: On event detail, find a recipe, click add note button (+ icon), enter note text, save, verify toast and note count updates
8.2 View Notes: Click 'Show More' to expand recipe card, verify notes section shows author name and note text
8.4 Edit Notes: Click edit (pencil) on a note, modify text, save, verify updated text visible
8.6 Delete Notes: Click delete (trash) on the note, verify confirmation dialog, confirm, verify note removed
Log all pass/fail results with screenshots to ralph/activity.md
If any test fails, diagnose the root cause and fix the code, then re-verify
userStories.notes: Navigate to an event detail page that has at least one recipe. The add note button is the '+' icon in the recipe card header. After adding a note, expand the card with 'Show More' to view it. Use a unique note text like 'E2E test note [timestamp]'. Clean up by deleting the note at the end. Skip 8.3 (multi-user), 8.5 (cancel delete — covered implicitly), 8.7-8.10 (file upload — requires actual files which is hard in browser automation).

userStories.title: E2E: Meal Planning grid & CRUD (Section 9)
userStories.description: As a QA tester, I want to verify the meal plan grid, week navigation, adding/editing/removing meals.
acceptanceCriteria: 9.1 Meal Plan Grid: Navigate to /dashboard/meals, verify 'Meal Plan' tab active, weekly grid shows 7 days x 3 meal types
9.2 Week Navigation: Click Next arrow, verify next week dates shown, click Previous, verify back, click Today, verify current week
9.3 Add Meal: Click '+' on an empty slot, enter a meal name on Custom tab, click Add Meal, verify meal appears
9.7 Remove Meal: Click remove button on the test meal, verify it's removed
Log all pass/fail results with screenshots to ralph/activity.md
If any test fails, diagnose the root cause and fix the code, then re-verify
userStories.notes: Navigate to /dashboard/meals. Use take_snapshot to find the grid structure, '+' buttons, and week navigation arrows. For adding a meal, find an empty slot's '+' button. Use a unique meal name. Clean up by removing the test meal. Skip 9.3a (manual ingredients), 9.4 (from recipes — needs existing recipes), 9.5 (upload), 9.6 (edit), 9.8 (action button labels), 9.9 (view details) — these are harder to automate or depend on specific state.

userStories.title: E2E: Meal Completion & Rating (Section 10)
userStories.description: As a QA tester, I want to verify marking meals as cooked, the rating dialog, and undo cook functionality.
acceptanceCriteria: 10.1 Mark Meal as Cooked (No Recipes): Add a custom meal (no URL), click 'Done', verify meal marked as cooked (green/checkmark)
10.3 Undo Cook: On the cooked meal, click 'Undo', verify confirmation dialog, click Continue, verify cook status removed
Clean up: Remove the test meal
Log all pass/fail results with screenshots to ralph/activity.md
If any test fails, diagnose the root cause and fix the code, then re-verify
userStories.notes: Navigate to /dashboard/meals. First add a custom meal without a URL (so 'Done' marks it cooked immediately without rating dialog). After marking as cooked, verify the visual state change. Then undo. Skip 10.2 (with recipes — rating dialog is complex), 10.4 (meal detail rating), 10.5/10.6 (edit from detail page). Clean up by removing the test meal at the end.

userStories.title: E2E: Meal Groceries (Section 11)
userStories.description: As a QA tester, I want to verify the meal plan groceries tab including combined view, pantry filtering, and week navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: 11.1 Groceries Tab: On Meals page, click 'Groceries' tab, verify week navigation still visible
11.6 Empty States: If no meals with recipes exist, verify appropriate empty state message displayed
11.8 Week Navigation: Switch weeks on Groceries tab, verify data reloads
Log all pass/fail results with screenshots to ralph/activity.md
If any test fails, diagnose the root cause and fix the code, then re-verify
userStories.notes: Navigate to /dashboard/meals then click 'Groceries' tab. The groceries tab may be empty if no meals have parsed recipes this week — verify the empty state message in that case. Skip 11.2-11.5 (combined/per-recipe/pantry — these need meals with parsed ingredients to test). Skip 11.7 (export — tested elsewhere).

userStories.title: E2E: Pantry management (Section 12)
userStories.description: As a QA tester, I want to verify the pantry dialog accessible from the header, including add/remove/duplicate-prevention.
acceptanceCriteria: 12.1 My Pantry Dialog: Click user menu in header, click 'My Pantry', verify pantry dialog opens with items
12.3 Add Item: Type a unique item name, press Enter, verify item added with toast 'Added [item] to pantry'
12.4 Duplicate Item: Try adding the same item again, verify error toast 'This item is already in your pantry'
12.5 Remove Item: Click trash icon on the test item, verify confirmation dialog, confirm, verify item removed
Log all pass/fail results with screenshots to ralph/activity.md
If any test fails, diagnose the root cause and fix the code, then re-verify
userStories.notes: From any dashboard page, click the user avatar/menu to find 'My Pantry'. Use a unique test item name like 'e2e-test-pantry-item'. After testing, remove it to clean up. Skip 12.2 (default items — can't test as first-time user) and 12.6 (grocery list integration — requires parsed recipes).

userStories.title: E2E: Admin features - User Management (Section 13)
userStories.description: As a QA tester, I want to verify the admin user management page including viewing users, adding, and role management.
acceptanceCriteria: 13.1 Manage Users - View: Click user menu > 'Manage Users', verify navigates to /users, user list displayed
13.7 Self-Protection: Verify your own user row has disabled role change / delete controls
Log all pass/fail results with screenshots to ralph/activity.md
If any test fails, diagnose the root cause and fix the code, then re-verify
userStories.notes: Login as admin (dev@example.com). Navigate via user menu > 'Manage Users'. Take a snapshot to verify the user list. Skip 13.2-13.6 (add/change/toggle/delete users — these modify real user data which is destructive for the test environment). Skip 13.8 (non-admin view — only one test user). For 13.7, just verify the current user's row shows disabled controls.

userStories.title: E2E: Responsive design (Section 15)
userStories.description: As a QA tester, I want to verify responsive layout at desktop, tablet, and mobile viewports.
acceptanceCriteria: 15.1 Desktop (1280px+): At 1280x800, verify header stats badges visible, tab labels visible, meal plan grid shows full 7-day view
15.2 Tablet (768px): Resize to 768x1024, verify header stats still visible
15.3 Mobile (<768px): Resize to 375x812, verify header stats hidden, tab labels shown below icons, meal plan grid has horizontal scroll
3.3 Mobile Tab Labels: At 375px, verify tab bar shows text labels below icons
3.5 Mobile Menu Stats: At 375px, click user menu, verify 'Club Events' and 'Club Recipes' in dropdown
Reset viewport to desktop size (1280x800) after testing
Log all pass/fail results with screenshots to ralph/activity.md
If any test fails, diagnose the root cause and fix the code, then re-verify
userStories.notes: Use mcp__chrome-devtools__resize_page to change viewport dimensions. Start at desktop (1280x800), then tablet (768x1024), then mobile (375x812). Take screenshots at each size. After testing, ALWAYS reset back to 1280x800. Use emulate tool with viewport settings for accurate mobile testing. Navigate to /dashboard first and login.

userStories.title: E2E: Accessibility checks (Section 16)
userStories.description: As a QA tester, I want to verify accessibility features like aria-labels, screen reader text, and touch targets.
acceptanceCriteria: 16.1 Icon Button Labels: Take a snapshot of the dashboard, verify icon-only buttons have aria-labels in the snapshot output
16.2 Color-Only Indicators: If a cooked meal exists, verify 'Cooked' text in snapshot (screen-reader-only)
16.4 Photo Alt Text: If recipe notes with photos exist, verify images have descriptive alt text
16.5 Star Rating: If a rating dialog can be opened, verify star buttons have aria-labels like 'Rate N out of 5 stars'
Log all pass/fail results with screenshots to ralph/activity.md
If any test fails, diagnose the root cause and fix the code, then re-verify
userStories.notes: Use take_snapshot with verbose: true to see all aria attributes. Navigate to various pages and check the snapshot output for aria-labels. For 16.2, navigate to /dashboard/meals and look for cooked meals. For 16.5, navigate to a completed event detail page and look for rating UI. Some checks may be 'SKIP — no data' if the test environment lacks specific state.

userStories.title: E2E: Email & Calendar in dev mode (Sections 17, 18)
userStories.description: As a QA tester, I want to verify that email notifications and Google Calendar integration gracefully handle dev mode (no API keys).
acceptanceCriteria: 17.4 Dev Mode Notifications: On an event detail page, add a test recipe, verify no error occurs during the notification step (silently succeeds)
18.4 Dev Mode Calendar: Verify no calendar errors appear in console when navigating event pages
Check browser console for any errors related to notifications or calendar on event pages
Clean up: Delete the test recipe
Log all pass/fail results with screenshots to ralph/activity.md
If any test fails, diagnose the root cause and fix the code, then re-verify
userStories.notes: Since we're in dev mode, email notifications are skipped and Google Calendar returns mock data. The main thing to verify is that NO ERRORS appear — the features degrade gracefully. Navigate to an event detail page, add a test recipe (manual, no URL), check browser console via list_console_messages for errors. Clean up by deleting the test recipe. Skip 17.1-17.3 (require real RESEND_API_KEY) and 18.1-18.3 (require real Google Calendar).

userStories.title: E2E: Generate test results summary
userStories.description: As a QA tester, I want a final summary of all E2E test results compiled from the activity log.
acceptanceCriteria: Read ralph/activity.md and compile results from all previous US-001 through US-019 entries
Create a summary table in ralph/activity.md with columns: Story, Tests Run, Passed, Failed, Skipped
List all bugs found and fixed during the test run
List all tests that were skipped and why
Provide an overall pass rate percentage
userStories.notes: This is a purely editorial story — no browser testing, no code changes. Read ralph/activity.md, parse the results from each previous story's log entry, and compile a summary. The summary should go at the TOP of activity.md under a '## E2E Test Results Summary' heading. Include a final recommendation: whether the app is ready for manual QA signoff or if critical issues remain.
